title: French Tender French Long Bean Rillettes
description: Sour and tender, this lazy Sunday dish is perfect for a Mediterranean-inspired meal featuring long-bean and yuzu-cure.

ingredients.name: Yuzu-cured pork belly
ingredients.notes: Yuzu cure must include yuzu juice, brown sugar, salt, and black pepper.

ingredients.name: White wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: White wine vinegar for a tangy flavor.

ingredients.name: Dijon mustard
ingredients.notes: For added depth, but adjust to taste.

instructions: Combine the yuzu-cured pork belly, long-bean, garlic, mustard, salt, and black pepper in a food processor. Process until coarsely chopped and evenly distributed.
Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the white wine vinegar and cook until the mixture thickens, about 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the Dijon mustard and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped mixture from the food processor and stir until well combined and heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and black pepper as needed.
Transfer the rillettes to a serving dish and garnish with chopped parsley.
Serve with sliced baguette and a side of yuzu juice for drizzling.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
For serving, stir the rillettes to redistribute the flavors before serving.
